core_mechanism: scaling limits of a measure-valued Markov jump process describing many interacting individuals, in which separately tuning system size, event/mutation rates, and jump-step size drives the microscopic stochastic dynamics to distinct macroscopic limiting regimes (deterministic laws, diffusion approximations, or slow jump processes)
brief_reason: The abstract centres on emerging scaling limits of a simple stochastic individual-based Markov-process model, with time, population size, mutation rate, and mutation step size as the scaling parameters that produce the various limiting regimes.
